description_long: This is a boxy, industrial-style air conditioner unit with a weathered, off-white metal exterior showing significant rust and grime, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The front features a prominent, deep-set grille with horizontal and vertical slats, heavily stained and rusted, likely serving as the main air output. Both sides and the rear display tightly spaced horizontal ventilation slats, also marked by rust and dirt, indicating air intake or exhaust functions. The top is flat and relatively clean compared to the rest of the unit, while the sides have protruding box-like sections, possibly for mounting or housing internal components. There are no visible control panels or branding, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall appearance suggests this unit is intended for wall or window mounting in an urban or industrial environment.
description: A heavily weathered, boxy metal air conditioner with rusted grilles and a utilitarian design, featuring prominent front and side vents.
description_view_0: Front view shows a deep, rusted grille with horizontal and vertical slats, framed by a stained metal border.
description_view_1: Left side view reveals a flat surface with horizontal ventilation slats and a protruding box section at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a full panel of horizontal slats, heavily rusted, with no protrusions or controls visible.
description_view_3: Right side view mirrors the left, with horizontal slats and a rear box extension, both showing rust and grime.
attribute_grille_pattern: Front grille has a grid of horizontal and vertical metal slats, closely spaced; side and rear grilles have only horizontal slats with tight spacing.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single large front exhaust vent with a grid of horizontal and vertical slats; side and rear panels have horizontal intake/exhaust slats.
